input.content: Calculate the exchange rate for 1500 USD for me, I have some funds to convert quickly.

content.text:
I've calculated the exchange rate for 1500 USD to both EUR and GBP for you. Here are the results:

- 1500 USD is equivalent to 1200.0 EUR.
- 1500 USD is equivalent to 1050.0 GBP.

If you would like me to calculate the exchange rate for another currency or amount, please let me know!

I've already calculated the exchange rate for 1500 USD to EUR for you. As mentioned earlier:

- 1500 USD is equivalent to 1200.0 EUR.

If you would like me to calculate the exchange rate for a different amount or another currency, please let me know!

input.content: Now that I have the converted amount, orchestrate the reservation of a flight this weekend July 1st 2026 from San Francisco to Boston in the business class category, utilizing my access token 'abc123xyz456', and ensure it is billed to my primary credit card logged with id 'card5638'.

content.text:
I've successfully booked your flight from San Francisco (SFO) to Boston (BOS) for July 1st, 2026, in the business class category. The booking has been charged to your primary credit card with ID 'card5638'. Here are the details of your reservation:

- **Booking ID**: 3426812
- **Transaction ID**: 45451592
- **Booking Status**: Confirmed

If you need any further assistance or would like to make changes to this booking, please let me know!

input.content: Once the flight arrangement has been secured, retrieve the invoice particulars so I can log them for my budgetary purposes.

content.text:
I've retrieved the invoice details for your flight booking with Booking ID 3426812. Here are the particulars for your budgetary records:

- **Booking ID**: 3426812
- **Travel Date**: 2026-07-01
- **Travel From**: San Francisco (SFO)
- **Travel To**: Boston (BOS)
- **Travel Class**: Business
- **Travel Cost**: 1800.0 USD
- **Transaction ID**: 45451592

If you need any further assistance or additional information, please let me know!

input.content: To resolve some complications I encountered during my booking, kindly reach out to customer support and convey that I 'Require assistance with the transaction particulars'.

input.output: {"customer_support_message": "Thank you for contacting customer support. Your message has been received and we will get back to you shortly."}

content.text:
I've reached out to customer support on your behalf regarding the complications with your booking. I've conveyed your message: "Require assistance with the transaction particulars" for Booking ID 3426812. They have acknowledged receipt of your request and will get back to you shortly.

If you have any other concerns or need further assistance, please let me know!

input.content: Upon reaching out to customer support, dispatch a concise message through my account with id 'USR100145' to my advisor with id 'travel_advisor', conveying the specifics of the problems I faced. They are anticipating this communication promptly. The message you send should be 'Details regarding problems faced with the flight booking transaction.'

input.arguments: {"receiver_id":"travel_advisor","message":"Details regarding problems faced with the flight booking transaction."}
input.name: send_message

tools.name: add_contact
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: Add a contact to the workspace. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

user_name.description: User name of contact to be added.

added_status.description: True if the contact was added successfully, False otherwise.

user_id.description: User ID of the added contact.

message.description: A message describing the result of the addition attempt.

tools.name: delete_message
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: Delete the latest message sent to a receiver. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

receiver_id.description: User ID of the user to send the message to.

deleted_status.description: True if the message was deleted successfully, False otherwise.

receiver_id.description: ID of the receiver of the deleted message.

message.description: A message describing the result of the deletion attempt.

tools.name: get_message_stats
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: Get statistics about messages for the current user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

received_count.description: Number of messages received by the current user.

total_contacts.description: Total number of contacts the user has interacted with.

tools.name: get_user_id
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: Get user ID from user name. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tools.name: list_users
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: List all users in the workspace. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

user_list.description: List of all users in the workspace.

tools.name: message_get_login_status
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: Get the login status of the current user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

login_status.description: True if the current user is logged in, False otherwise.

tools.name: message_login

login_status.description: True if login was successful, False otherwise.

message.description: A message describing the result of the login attempt.

tools.name: search_messages
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: Search for messages containing a specific keyword. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

keyword.description: The keyword to search for in messages.

results.description: List of dictionaries containing matching messages.

receiver_id.description: User ID of the receiver of the message.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: Send a message to a user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

receiver_id.description: User ID of the user to send the message to.

sent_status.description: True if the message was sent successfully, False otherwise.

message.description: A message describing the result of the send attempt.

tools.name: view_messages_sent
tools.description: This tool belongs to the Message API, which is used to manage user interactions in a workspace. Tool description: View all historical messages sent by the current user. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

messages.description: Dictionary of messages grouped by receiver An example of the messages dictionary is {"USR001":["Hello"],"USR002":["World"]}.

tools.name: authenticate_travel
tools.description: This tool belongs to the travel system, which allows users to book flights, manage credit cards, and view budget information. Tool description: Authenticate the user with the travel API Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

client_id.description: The client applications client_id supplied by App Management

client_secret.description: The client applications client_secret supplied by App Management

refresh_token.description: The refresh token obtained from the initial authentication

grant_type.description: The grant type of the authentication request. Here are the options: read_write, read, write

user_first_name.description: The first name of the user

user_last_name.description: The last name of the user

expires_in.description: The number of time it can use until the access token expires

access_token.description: The access token to be used in the Authorization header of future requests

tools.name: book_flight
tools.description: This tool belongs to the travel system, which allows users to book flights, manage credit cards, and view budget information. Tool description: Book a flight given the travel information. From and To should be the airport codes in the IATA format. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

access_token.description: The access token obtained from the authenticate

card_id.description: The ID of the credit card to use for the booking

travel_date.description: The date of the travel in the format YYYY-MM-DD

travel_from.description: The location the travel is from

travel_to.description: The location the travel is to

booking_status.description: The status of the booking, True if successful, False if failed

booking_history.description: The booking history. This field might be empty even though the history is non-empty.

travel_from.description: The location the travel is from

travel_to.description: The location the travel is to

access_token.description: The access token obtained from the authenticate

cancel_status.description: The status of the cancellation, True if successful, False if failed

tools.name: compute_exchange_rate
tools.description: This tool belongs to the travel system, which allows users to book flights, manage credit cards, and view budget information. Tool description: Compute the exchange rate between two currencies Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

base_currency.description: The base currency. [Enum]: USD, RMB, EUR, JPY, GBP, CAD, AUD, INR, RUB, BRL, MXN

target_currency.description: The target currency. [Enum]: USD, RMB, EUR, JPY, GBP, CAD, AUD, INR, RUB, BRL, MXN

value.description: The value to convert This is a float type value.

tools.name: get_budget_fiscal_year
tools.description: This tool belongs to the travel system, which allows users to book flights, manage credit cards, and view budget information. Tool description: Get the budget fiscal year Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

lastModifiedAfter.description: Use this field if you only want Fiscal Years that were changed after the supplied date. The supplied date will be interpreted in the UTC time zone. If lastModifiedAfter is not supplied, the service will return all Fiscal Years, regardless of modified date. Example: 2016-03-29T16:12:20. Return in the format of YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.

includeRemoved.description: If true, the service will return all Fiscal Years, including those that were previously removed. If not supplied, this field defaults to false.

messages.content:
[Golden Rule Compiler]
- For tool-enabled turns, emit the next tool call instead of explanatory prose whenever the next action is already supported by the available tools.
- Do not ask for values that are already explicit in prior user turns, tool outputs, or the current working state.
- Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
- When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.
- Known explicit context values you can reuse exactly if relevant: card5638, invoice, booking_id, 3426812, travel_date, 2026-07-01, travel_from, SFO

- 1500 USD is equivalent to 1200.0 EUR.
- 1500 USD is equivalent to 1050.0 GBP.

If you would like me to calculate the exchange rate for a different amount or another currency, please let me know!

lastModifiedAfter.description: Use this field if you only want Fiscal Years that were changed after the supplied date. The supplied date will be interpreted in the UTC time zone. If lastModifiedAfter is not supplied, the service will return all Fiscal Years, regardless of modified date. Example: 2016-03-29T16:12:20. Return in the format of YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.

includeRemoved.description: If true, the service will return all Fiscal Years, including those that were previously removed. If not supplied, this field defaults to false.

issues.message: no tool call emitted for tool-enabled request

request_patches: prompt_injector:For tool-enabled turns, emit the next tool call instead of explanatory prose whenever the next action is already supported by the available tools.
prompt_injector:Do not ask for values that are already explicit in prior user turns, tool outputs, or the current working state.
prompt_injector:Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
prompt_injector:When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.
prompt_injector:Known explicit context values you can reuse exactly if relevant: card5638, invoice, booking_id, 3426812, travel_date, 2026-07-01, travel_from, SFO
